mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and higher corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth correlates with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of expansion phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with accelerating inflation erode real yields and force aggressive monetary tightening, triggering sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of high rates and rising inflation fails to predict negative 5-day returns (win rate < 40% in triggered samples).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue streams and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sudden labor market weakness and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months following a payroll decline exceeding 100,000 jobs.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals robust economic momentum and strong earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive divergence signal yields negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed cases.
